title: salmalia scopulorum
intro: Salmalia scopulorum, also known as the Indian laurel, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its broad leaves and showy white flowers. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Salmalia scopulorum include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to create medicinal products such as tonics, syrups, and ointments.
